# Account Payment Return Import Iso20022

{{ badge1 }} {{ badge2 }} {{ badge3 }} {{ badge4 }} {{ badge5 }}

Module to import payment returns from pain.002.001.03 and
camt.054.001.02 files.

**Table of contents**

```{contents}
:local: true
```

## Installation

This module depends on *account_payment_order* module from
OCA/bank-payment repository.

## Bug Tracker

Bugs are tracked on [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/OCA/account-payment/issues).
In case of trouble, please check there if your issue has already been reported.
If you spotted it first, help us to smash it by providing a detailed and welcomed
[feedback](https://github.com/OCA/account-payment/issues/new?body=module:%20account_payment_return_import_iso20022%0Aversion:%2017.0%0A%0A**Steps%20to%20reproduce**%0A-%20...%0A%0A**Current%20behavior**%0A%0A**Expected%20behavior**).

Do not contact contributors directly about support or help with technical issues.

Try to parse the file as the following format or fall back on next
parser:
    - camt.054.001.02
    - pain.002.001.03

classes.name: odoo.addons.account_payment_return_import_iso20022.wizard.pain_parser.PainParser

methods.name: odoo.addons.account_payment_return_import_iso20022.wizard.pain_parser.PainParser.parse_amount
documentation: Parse element that contains Amount and CreditDebitIndicator.

methods.name: odoo.addons.account_payment_return_import_iso20022.wizard.pain_parser.PainParser.add_value_from_node
documentation:
Add value to object from first or all nodes found with xpath.

If xpath_str is a list (or iterable), it will be seen as a series
of search path's in order of preference. The first item that results
in a found node will be used to set a value in the dictionary.

methods.name: odoo.addons.account_payment_return_import_iso20022.wizard.pain_parser.PainParser.parse_payment_return
documentation: Parse a single payment return node.

documentation: Validate validity of SEPA Direct Debit Unpaid Report file.
Parser for SEPA Direct Debit Unpaid Report import files.

documentation:
Add value to object from first or all nodes found with xpath.

If xpath_str is a list (or iterable), it will be seen as a series
of search path's in order of preference. The first item that results
in a found node will be used to set a value in the dictionary.
